chapter_context.summary: Satan incites David to number Israel; God is displeased and sends a three-day plague that kills 70,000; David repents and purchases Ornan's threshing floor to offer sacrifices.

outline.text: Satan Stirs Up David to Number Israel; Joab Reluctantly Complies
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Satan Stirs Up David to Number Israel; Joab Reluctantly Complies (21:1-6), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 1 Chronicles.

outline.text: God Displeased; David Chooses Three Days of Pestilence
outline.explanation: This section focuses on God Displeased; David Chooses Three Days of Pestilence (21:7-13), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 1 Chronicles.

outline.text: Plague Smites 70000; David Intercedes as Angel Stands by Threshing Floor
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Plague Smites 70000; David Intercedes as Angel Stands by Threshing Floor (21:14-17), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 1 Chronicles.

outline.text: David Buys Ornan's Threshing Floor; Offers Sacrifices; Fire from Heaven
outline.explanation: This section focuses on David Buys Ornan's Threshing Floor; Offers Sacrifices; Fire from Heaven (21:18-30), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 1 Chronicles.

key_themes.title: Covenant Lineage
key_themes.description: The meticulous genealogies show that God's plan is continuous and links post-exilic Israel back to creation.

key_themes.title: The Throne of David
key_themes.description: David's rise and worship preparations focus on establishing Jerusalem as the central theological hub of the kingdom.

devotional.question: Reflecting on the events of Chapter 21, how does seeing God's faithfulness through history encourage you today?
How does this chapter prompt you to dedicate your personal plans and gifts to the LORD's work?

cultural_historical.title: The Post-Exilic Restoration
cultural_historical.text: Chronicles was written for the returning exiles to rebuild their religious identity, focusing heavily on priestly lineages and temple worship.
